title: Hesam Oveys
entries: Curves the exam well and you can find similar questions on the lectures

Good professor, very kind and understanding. However, final was HARD. Like it was a lot harder than both midterms. I did well on both midterms, which I think it pretty doable if you just review class examples and quizzes. HW is unrelated to exams. He rushed a lot during the end of the semester so I felt very unprepared. But a good prof!

tough grader b/c of the TAs & administrator. final was EXTREMELY hard but won't blame oveys b/c the test was made by administrator. wish he prepped us more for it though. weekly quizzes but is generous, allows us to drop the 3 lowest quizzes. pretty generous with end curve. homework felt unrelated though. records lectures and gives class notes.

Enthusiastic about math but exams very hard he will say oh exams are similar to notes because he probably reads the reviews but it ain't also heard other set midterm was easier or different don't understand why

Professor Oveys is a very good lecturer and is easy to understand. However, after talking with several students in the other set. it became clear that their exam was a lot easier. The average between sets was very different, with the other set being much higher. As a student this feels unfair and the curve between both sets should not be the same.

Tests are so hard for no reason. He always says “they should be easy if you study right” but don't listen, it will be difficult no matter how much you study. He's obviously passionate about math, but makes people not want to ask questions since he usually shuts down the question and makes them feel ashamed for asking.

Yes this class is hard but so are all the other calc teacher's classes. When you compare him to other professors, his teaching is MUCH better. The exams are difficult, and they mirror the hardest class example problems as opposed to the more average problems. However, all of these difficult problems are similar-ish to ones in the notes.

He is a very caring and kind professor, exams are hard. But he tries to help you and your grades rather than lowering them or making you fail. We had a medical emergency occur in the exam and he was very flexible allowing exam weight transfers etc.
Very kind professor very good teacher but the exams are very difficult . It's like the exam came from a different planet like planet mars .
Good professor, explains clearly and wants you to learn. However, exams are VERY hard and if you study from his notes and practice problems you still will not do good.

He's a phenomenal teacher. Records all lectures, releases his notes and extra practice problems, and textbook is optional. Overall Hesam was great. You must be able to do all the problems from his notes to pass his exams. Just do the problems, and the exams aren't bad at all. They're the same problems. HW is hard too, but it's HW and not on exams.

The homework and quizzes ask you to solve 1+1. The exam asks you to solve cancer. Very hard exams, even if you study from his notes you will still be surprised that in the exam you will find stuff that you do not know how to solve and start questioning whether you took it before or not.

If you need to meet the math requirement for the Econ major, take the Calc sequence. Even though it goes through Calc 3, I'd still choose Calc II over this class. The professor is passionate and clear about grading, but can come off egocentric - more focused on lecturing than student success. If that works for you, stick with him.

Honestly, people give such low ratings makes me wanna write this. Yes midterms and final are hard, but everthing on exmas are covered in the course. Apart from the exams everyone complain about, this guy gives the best Math lectures I've ever had in my life. He is so passionate and sophisticated in what he teach. Will definitely take his class more
Very hard exams that he does not prepare you to take. What he teaches in class is different from what is on the midterm and final despite what he may say.

difficulty of the tests is much higher than what is taught in class. Oveys' class also received less time for the midterm than other classes taught by other professors. He kept telling us to review from class notes and quizzes, which were not that helpful because they didnt rlly match the difficulty of the final
so much harder than MFE1, oveys is fine as a professor but MFE2 in general is just a difficult class
Great professor, watch his lectures and go through the types of question in his homework quizzes and recitation sheets

You can take online course and read his notes, which covers everything. The practice example cover every single part of course so that even though you don't take class sometimes, you can also get very high score in exam. Homework is kind of heavy to be honest. In this course, you need to take time and do more practice.

Oveys has a clear passion for maths and teaching but I'm not sure if he's the optimal choice for all students. Personally, I struggled with his methods despite studying and scored poorly on the exams. He definitely knows his stuff but he doesn't do the best at teaching them at all times. Also which TA you have matters in your weekly quiz grades.
